question: Can real GW event posteriors be explored with the value/shape geometry framework?

data_sources.note: Raw HDF5 posterior files are ignored by git; only derived geometry is committed.

supported: Actual GW posterior chains can be reduced to the same value/shape geometry objects used in the cosmology atlas.
GW190814 is geometrically sharp and asymmetric: its mass plane has rho=-0.945 and axis_ratio=36.73, while mass-ratio/spin has rho=-0.955.
GW190521_074359 provides a high-mass BBH geometry anchor, including final-mass/final-spin rho=0.322 and axis_ratio=103.75.
disfavored: The idea that all compact-binary events have interchangeable posterior geometry.
A first-pass claim of quantum-gravity or no-hair violation; E38 is an observational-geometry test, not a GR-deviation fit.
open: Extend to more posterior HDF5 files from GWOSC/Zenodo and run event-family clustering.
For actual GR tests, ingest LVK inspiral-merger-ringdown, ringdown, dispersion, polarization, or parameterized-deviation products.
For cosmological gravity, use standard-siren distance-redshift likelihoods rather than BBH mass/spin geometry.
bottom_line: Supported: real GW posterior samples can be mapped into the same geometry language, and landmark events occupy visibly different black-hole geometry regimes. Not supported: any claim of quantum-gravity or GR violation from this first geometry pass.
